connectorUiConfig.title: Vaikora AI Agent Behavioral Signals
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Ingest AI agent behavioral signals from the Vaikora API into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). Monitor agent actions, policy decisions, anomaly scores, and risk levels to detect suspicious AI activity in your environment.

sampleQueries.query: Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h) | where severity_s in ('high', 'critical') | project TimeGenerated, agent_id_s, action_type_s, severity_s, policy_decision_s, anomaly_score_d

Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | where is_anomaly_b == true | summarize AnomalyCount=count(), AvgAnomalyScore=avg(anomaly_score_d) by agent_id_s, action_type_s | order by AnomalyCount desc
Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(48h) | where policy_decision_s == 'block' | project TimeGenerated, agent_id_s, action_type_s, resource_type_s, policy_id_s, log_hash_s

dataTypes.name: Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery: Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated) | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: Vaikora API Key
customs.description: A Vaikora API key (vk_xxxxx) with read access to the actions endpoint. Obtain this from your Vaikora dashboard under Settings > API Keys.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Vaikora AI Agent Behavioral Signals
instructionSteps.description:
To enable the Vaikora connector, enter your Vaikora API key below and click Connect. The Agent ID is optional; use it to scope ingestion to a single agent, or leave it blank to ingest actions from all agents the key can see.

Your API key is available in the [Vaikora dashboard](https://vaikora.com) under **Settings > API Keys**. The Agent ID is the UUID shown on each agent's detail page.

parameters.label: Vaikora Agent ID (optional)
parameters.placeholder: Leave blank to monitor all agents
parameters.name: vaikoraAgentId

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend a = todynamic(action) | extend TimeGenerated = todatetime(a['timestamp']), action_id_s = tostring(a['id']), agent_id_s = tostring(a['agent_id']), action_type_s = tostring(a['action_type']), resource_type_s = tostring(a['resource_type']), status_s = tostring(a['status']), severity_s = tostring(a['severity']), policy_id_s = tostring(a['policy_id']), policy_decision_s = tostring(a['policy_decision']), is_anomaly_b = tobool(a['is_anomaly']), anomaly_score_d = toreal(a['anomaly_score']), anomaly_reason_s = tostring(a['anomaly_reason']), log_hash_s = tostring(a['log_hash']) | project TimeGenerated, action_id_s, agent_id_s, action_type_s, resource_type_s, status_s, severity_s, policy_id_s, policy_decision_s, is_anomaly_b, anomaly_score_d, anomaly_reason_s, log_hash_s

connectorUiConfig.title: Vaikora AI Agent Behavioral Signals
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Ingest AI agent behavioral signals from the Vaikora API into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). Monitor agent actions, policy decisions, anomaly scores, and risk levels to detect suspicious AI activity in your environment.

sampleQueries.query: Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h) | where severity_s in ('high', 'critical') | project TimeGenerated, agent_id_s, action_type_s, severity_s, policy_decision_s, anomaly_score_d

Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | where is_anomaly_b == true | summarize AnomalyCount=count(), AvgAnomalyScore=avg(anomaly_score_d) by agent_id_s, action_type_s | order by AnomalyCount desc
Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(48h) | where policy_decision_s == 'block' | project TimeGenerated, agent_id_s, action_type_s, resource_type_s, policy_id_s, log_hash_s

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: Vaikora API Key
customs.description: A Vaikora API key (vk_xxxxx) with read access to the actions endpoint. Obtain this from your Vaikora dashboard under Settings > API Keys.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Vaikora AI Agent Behavioral Signals
instructionSteps.description:
To enable the Vaikora connector, enter your Vaikora API key below and click Connect. The Agent ID is optional; use it to scope ingestion to a single agent, or leave it blank to ingest actions from all agents the key can see.

Your API key is available in the [Vaikora dashboard](https://vaikora.com) under **Settings > API Keys**. The Agent ID is the UUID shown on each agent's detail page.

parameters.label: Vaikora Agent ID (optional)
parameters.placeholder: Leave blank to monitor all agents
parameters.name: vaikoraAgentId

properties.description: Vaikora - High Risk AI Agent Action_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0
Identifies AI agent actions from Vaikora classified as high or critical severity. These events may indicate an agent operating outside safe parameters or triggering policy thresholds.
properties.displayName: Vaikora - High severity AI agent action detected
properties.query:
Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where severity_s in ("high", "critical")
| summarize
    ActionCount = count(),
    MaxAnomalyScore = max(anomaly_score_d),
    Actions = make_set(action_type_s),
    PolicyDecisions = make_set(policy_decision_s),
    ResourceTypes = make_set(resource_type_s),
    LogHashes = make_set(log_hash_s)
  by AgentId = agent_id_s, Severity = severity_s
| extend
    ActionList = strcat_array(Actions, ", "),
    PolicyList = strcat_array(PolicyDecisions, ", "),
    ResourceList = strcat_array(ResourceTypes, ", ")

properties.displayName: Vaikora - High severity AI agent action detected

properties.description: Vaikora - Behavioral Anomaly Detected_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0
Identifies AI agent behavioral anomalies flagged by Vaikora with an anomaly score of 0.7 or above, indicating significant deviation from the agent's established behavioral baseline.
properties.query:
Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where is_anomaly_b == true
| where anomaly_score_d >= 0.7
| summarize
    AnomalyCount = count(),
    MaxAnomalyScore = max(anomaly_score_d),
    AvgAnomalyScore = avg(anomaly_score_d),
    AnomalyReasons = make_set(anomaly_reason_s),
    ActionTypes = make_set(action_type_s)
  by AgentId = agent_id_s, Severity = severity_s
| extend
    ReasonList = strcat_array(AnomalyReasons, "; "),
    ActionList = strcat_array(ActionTypes, ", ")

properties.description: Vaikora - Agent Policy Violation_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0
Identifies AI agent actions explicitly blocked by a Vaikora policy. Repeated violations from the same agent may indicate prompt injection, policy circumvention, or a compromised workflow.
properties.query:
Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where policy_decision_s == "block"
| summarize
    ViolationCount = count(),
    PolicyIds = make_set(policy_id_s),
    ActionTypes = make_set(action_type_s),
    ResourceTypes = make_set(resource_type_s),
    MaxAnomalyScore = max(anomaly_score_d),
    Severities = make_set(severity_s),
    LogHashes = make_set(log_hash_s)
  by AgentId = agent_id_s
| extend
    PolicyList = strcat_array(PolicyIds, ", "),
    ActionList = strcat_array(ActionTypes, ", "),
    ResourceList = strcat_array(ResourceTypes, ", ")
| where ViolationCount >= 1

metadata.description: Visualization of Vaikora AI agent behavioral signals including signal overview, action timelines, severity breakdowns, anomaly detection, and policy violations for SecOps teams.

resources.description: This workbook provides visualization and monitoring for Vaikora AI agent behavioral signals including action timelines, severity breakdowns, anomaly detection, and policy violations.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Vaikora AI Agent Behavioral Signals solution integrates Vaikora AI agent behavioral data into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). The solution deploys a REST API poller connector, a custom log table (Vaikora_AgentSignals_CL), analytics rules, and visualization workbook to help security teams monitor AI agent activity, detect behavioral anomalies, and investigate policy violations.

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 3

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
